// Phase 5 D-05 — bytecode source for `revert_counter.hex`.
//
// This contract reverts UNCONDITIONALLY on any incoming call (no function
// dispatch — every call to the runtime body REVERTs with a standard
// `Error(string)` payload). simulate_one tests target it via any selector.
//
// The bytecode is hand-assembled (no solc dep) — it is small enough that
// inline assembly is clearer than a Solidity build pipeline. Audit by
// disassembling `revert_counter.hex`:
//
//   Constructor (deployer, 12 bytes — returns runtime body):
//     PUSH1 0x70        ; runtime length = 112
//     PUSH1 0x0c        ; runtime offset within constructor code
//     PUSH1 0x00        ; dest mem
//     CODECOPY          ; mem[0..112] = code[12..124]
//     PUSH1 0x70        ; length
//     PUSH1 0x00        ; offset
//     RETURN
//
//   Runtime (12 bytes preamble + 100 bytes embedded revert data):
//     PUSH1 0x64        ; revert-data length = 100
//     PUSH1 0x0c        ; data offset within runtime code (after preamble)
//     PUSH1 0x00        ; dest mem
//     CODECOPY          ; mem[0..100] = revert payload
//     PUSH1 0x64        ; length
//     PUSH1 0x00        ; offset
//     REVERT            ; revert with mem[0..100]
//
//   Embedded revert data (100 bytes — `Error(string)` ABI-encoded):
//     selector  : 0x08c379a0
//     offset    : 0x00..0020 (32-byte BE = 32)
//     length    : 0x00..0014 (32-byte BE = 20)
//     "forced revert reason" (20 bytes) padded right with 12 zero bytes
//
// Equivalent Solidity:
//   // SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT
//   pragma solidity ^0.8.20;
//   contract RevertCounter {
//       fallback() external payable {
//           revert("forced revert reason");
//       }
//       receive() external payable {
//           revert("forced revert reason");
//       }
//   }
//
// (The Solidity version compiles to ~250 bytes after function-dispatch
// preamble; the hand-assembled version is 124 bytes total.)
